content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662         322-5660          322-5901          322-6441


December 20, 1984


Roy Lockwood
P.O. Box 1306
Oxnard, CA 93032

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-84-308

Dear Mr. Lockwood:

We have received your letter concerning Oxnard City
Councilmember Michael A. Plisky. As I explained in our
telephone conversation, the Fair Political Practices Commission
issues specific advice to a person only with respect to that
person's duties under the Political Reform Act. Therefore, I
have not answered your questions about what would constitute a
conflict of interest for Councilmember Plisky. I have, however,
enclosed a "Guide to the Political Reform Act" which will help
you to understand the general conflict of interest provisions of
the Political Reform Act.

In our telephone conversation, you stated that you recently
sent two additional letters regarding Councilmember Plisky
acting on matters in which you believe he has a financial
interest. These letters will be referred to our Enforcement
Division, as will any additional information we receive from you
regarding possible violations of the Political Reform Act. I
have enclosed a copy of our complaint form, which you may use in
informing us of possible violations.

Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan
Counsel
Legal Division

KED:nwm
Enclosures

ROY W. LOCKWOOD
P.O. BOX 1306
OXNARD, CA. 93032
/2-6-84

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, Calif. 95814

Michael A. Plisky has been elected to the Oxnard City Council.
In his campaign statement he lists his occupation as a businessman. However it is believed he is an Accountant and advises and counsels clients of his who are businessmen and members of the Chamber of Commerce. He is also a Past President of the Oxnard Chamber of Commerce.

Whenever any of his past or present clients who he has counseled or advised of managed their financial accounts appears before the City Council where he sits as a Councilman; would Mr. Plisky have to disqualify himself and abstain from voting on their matters?

Thank you for your cooperation in regard to answering and giving a decision on the above.

Sincerely yours,

Roy W. Lockwood

1 Attachment:
   1. Campaign Statement of
      Michael Plisky.

CANDIDATE FOR MEMBER OF THE CITY COUNCIL
CITY OF OXNARD

NAME: MICHAEL A. "MIKE" PLISKY                                    AGE: 42
OCCUPATION: Oxnard Businessman
EDUCATION AND PERSONAL HISTORY:

    Graduate of USC 1969 with a B.S. Degree in Business Management. Life member of
Alpha Kappa Psi. Resident and businessman in Oxnard past 11 years. Three children
- Michael, 23; Adrienne, 21; Yvette, 20. Member Santa Clara Parsh.

COMMUNITY INVOLVEMENT:

    Oxnard Chamber of Commerce, Vice-President 1978, 1980-81; President-Elect 1982;
President 1983 and 1984. Oxnard Union High School Foundation, Board of Directors
1983-84. Ventura County United Way, Oxnard Vice-Chairman 1984-85. Oxnard Convention and Visitors Bureau, Board of Directors 1983-85.

STATEMENT:

    IT'S TIME FOR A CHANGE! Oxnard is one of the fastest growing cities in the United
States. Poor planning and bad management have created a dilapidated infrastructure
system, overcrowded schools, and the necessity to impose a burdensome utility tax.
We need someone on the City Council who has fresh new ideas, foresight, courage,
and management abilities. I believe my education and training provide me with these
skills. Working together, we can return to a responsible fiscal program within the
constraints imposed by Proposition 13. Together, we can conquer the problems
facing Oxnard today so that our children will enjoy a truly fine quality of life in
Oxnard tomorrow.

    I would appreciate your support. Thank you.

                                        s/ Michael A. Plisky

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance
(916) 322-5662

Administration
322-5660

Executive/Legal
322-5901

Enforcement
322-6441

December 14, 1984


Roy W. Lockwood
P.O. Box 1306
Oxnard, CA 93032

Re: A-84-308

Dear Mr. Lockwood:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Kathryn Donovan, an
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
General Counsel

BAM:plh

sections.conclusion: The Fair Political Practices Commission issues specific advice only to individuals concerning their own duties under the Political Reform Act, and therefore declined to answer questions about what would constitute a conflict of interest for Councilmember Plisky. The Commission referred the requester's letters concerning possible violations to the Enforcement Division.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester sought advice regarding whether Oxnard City Councilmember Michael A. Plisky had conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act based on matters he acted upon.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC declined to provide specific advice about another person's conflict of interest, as it only provides advice to individuals about their own duties, but referred the matters to the Enforcement Division for investigation.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two related letters: an initial acknowledgment letter (December 14, 1984) and the substantive response letter (December 20, 1984) from Counsel. The document also includes an unrelated candidate profile page for Michael A. Plisky. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers were present, but the substance of both is evident in the letter text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester sought advice regarding whether Oxnard City Councilmember Michael A. Plisky had conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act based on matters he acted upon.

CONCLUSION: The Fair Political Practices Commission issues specific advice only to individuals concerning their own duties under the Political Reform Act, and therefore declined to answer questions about what would constitute a conflict of interest for Councilmember Plisky. The Commission referred the requester's letters concerning possible violations to the Enforcement Division.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide advice about City Councilmember Plisky's potential conflicts of interest, explaining that it only provides specific advice to individuals about their own duties. The Commission referred the requester's allegations of possible violations to the Enforcement Division and provided guidance materials on the Political Reform Act.
